story.iWant: clear assurance that my presets are private
story.soThat: I feel confident Recipe won't leak my proprietary editing styles to competitors or third parties

tasks.task: Create privacy-messaging.js module for privacy education
Add privacy badge to header (🔒 100% Privacy - Your files never leave your device)
Create Privacy FAQ section with 6 Q&A items (collapsible)
Add privacy badge click handler (scroll to FAQ)
Add GitHub link in footer (open source transparency)
Implement privacy status message container (auto-fade after 5s)
Add CSS styling for privacy badge, FAQ, status messages
Add HTML elements for privacy badge, FAQ, footer links
Integrate with fileLoaded event (show privacy reminder)
Integrate with conversionComplete event (show privacy message)
Verify no analytics/tracking in DevTools Network tab

criterion.title: Privacy Badge in Header
criterion.requirement: Display privacy badge below main title
Badge text: "🔒 100% Privacy - Your files never leave your device"
Badge style: Green background (#c6f6d5), subtle, always visible
Badge clickable: scrolls to Privacy FAQ section
criterion.test: Load page, verify privacy badge visible in header, click badge → scrolls to FAQ section

criterion.title: Privacy Messaging on File Upload
criterion.requirement: Show privacy reminder when file is uploaded: "✓ File loaded. Processing locally in your browser - no server upload."
Display briefly (3-5 seconds), then fade out
Subtle, non-intrusive (doesn't interrupt workflow)
criterion.test: Upload file, verify privacy message appears after file loads, fades out after ~3-5 seconds

criterion.title: Privacy Reminder After Conversion
criterion.requirement: Include privacy message in conversion success: "✓ Conversion complete! Your preset was converted entirely in your browser."
Emphasize no data uploaded or stored
Optional link to Privacy FAQ
criterion.test: Complete conversion flow, verify success message includes privacy reminder

criterion.title: Privacy FAQ Section
criterion.requirement: Create expandable FAQ section in footer (collapsible)
6 Q&A items covering: server uploads, file tracking, WASM explanation, auditability, analytics, browser safety
FAQ toggle button: "Privacy FAQ ▼" → "Hide Privacy FAQ ▲"
Link from privacy badge to FAQ (scroll + auto-expand)
criterion.test: Scroll to footer, click "Privacy FAQ ▼" → section expands, read all Q&A, click badge in header → jumps to FAQ

criterion.title: Technical Transparency (Explain WASM)
criterion.requirement: Brief WebAssembly explanation in FAQ: "Recipe uses WebAssembly (WASM) to run the conversion engine in your browser. WASM is a safe, sandboxed technology..."
Link to external WebAssembly resource (https://webassembly.org/)
Avoid technical jargon (plain language for non-technical users)
criterion.test: Read WASM explanation in FAQ, verify understandable to non-technical users, click external link → opens in new tab

criterion.title: Open Source Transparency
criterion.requirement: Display GitHub link in footer: "📂 Open Source - View the code on GitHub"
Emphasize auditability in FAQ: "All Recipe code is public. You can verify exactly what it does."
Link to Recipe repository (opens in new tab)
criterion.test: Click GitHub link in footer, verify opens Recipe repository in new tab, repository is public

criterion.title: No Tracking or Analytics
criterion.requirement: Option 1 (Preferred): No analytics at all
Never use third-party trackers (Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, etc.)
FAQ explicitly states: "Recipe doesn't use tracking or analytics. No cookies, no telemetry, no fingerprinting."
criterion.test: Open DevTools → Network tab, load page, complete conversion flow, verify no requests to analytics services, no cookies set

criterion.title: Privacy Policy (Optional)
criterion.requirement: Optional: Add minimal privacy policy page or section
Content: "Recipe doesn't collect, store, or transmit your files. Recipe doesn't use cookies or tracking. Recipe is a static web application."
Link from footer (if implemented)
criterion.test: If implemented: Click "Privacy Policy" link in footer, verify policy page loads and matches actual behavior

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Simple story (0.5-1 day). Deliverables: Privacy badge in header, privacy reminders (after upload, after conversion), Privacy FAQ section (6 Q&A). Emphasizes WASM-based local processing (no server uploads). Open source transparency (GitHub link). No analytics or tracking.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Epic 2 transforms Recipe from a Go library into a browser-based photo preset converter using WebAssembly. This epic delivers the MVP web interface enabling photographers to convert presets without installing software, with 100% client-side processing for privacy.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Zero server communication - all processing happens in the browser via WebAssembly.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Privacy is Recipe's core value proposition. Validation strategy: Open DevTools Network tab, complete full conversion flow, verify: No XHR/fetch requests (only WASM load), No cookies set, No local storage used, All conversion happens in browser memory.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Clear privacy assurance to build trust. Privacy is Recipe's core differentiator. Photographers treat presets as trade secrets worth thousands of dollars. Recipe (files never leave device, WASM in browser) vs Competitors (upload to server, privacy risk, GDPR concerns). Trust factors: Clear privacy messaging, technical transparency (explain WASM), open source auditability.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Recipe is designed for privacy from the ground up. No servers, no databases, no accounts. All processing happens in the browser using WebAssembly. Your files never leave your device.

artifact.title: Architecture
artifact.snippet: Recipe compiles to WebAssembly for browser execution. WASM provides: Near-native performance, sandboxed security, cross-platform compatibility. All conversion logic runs client-side in browser memory. Zero server communication ensures absolute privacy.

artifact.title: Architecture
artifact.snippet: Recipe's architecture ensures privacy: Client-side processing (no server uploads), No persistence (files only in memory), No tracking or analytics, Open source (auditable code). WebAssembly runs in sandboxed environment with same security as JavaScript.

artifact.title: Story 2-6: WASM Conversion Execution
artifact.snippet: WebAssembly runs in sandboxed environment. Cannot access filesystem, network, or other browser tabs without explicit permission. Same security model as JavaScript.

artifact.reason: Story 2-2 dispatches fileLoaded event when file uploaded. Privacy reminder should display after this event.
Story 2-6 dispatches conversionComplete event after successful conversion. Privacy message should display after this event.
Central integration point. Will add event listeners for fileLoaded and conversionComplete to trigger privacy messaging.
Privacy badge will be added to header section. Privacy FAQ and GitHub link will be added to footer section.
Styling for privacy badge (green theme), FAQ section (collapsible), privacy status messages (fade animation).
WASM loading logic demonstrates privacy (no server uploads). Can reference in FAQ technical explanation.

javascript.package: getElementById, addEventListener, scrollIntoView for privacy interactions

browser.requirement: Required for privacy badge click → scroll to FAQ
Required for fade-out animation on privacy reminders

story-dependencies.story: Provides header structure for privacy badge placement
Provides fileLoaded event for privacy reminder trigger
Provides conversionComplete event for privacy message, demonstrates WASM privacy
Download completes privacy story (upload → process → download, all local)

constraints.constraint: CRITICAL: Zero tracking or analytics - no third-party scripts, no cookies, no telemetry
All processing client-side - no server communication except initial page/WASM load
Privacy messages non-technical - plain language understandable by photographers
Privacy FAQ must be accurate - technical claims must match implementation
Privacy badge always visible in header (not scrolled away)
Privacy reminders subtle and non-intrusive (auto-fade, don't block workflow)
Privacy FAQ collapsible (hidden by default, expand on click)
Open source link prominent in footer
WASM explanation accurate but non-technical (avoid jargon like "sandboxed", "marshaling")
Privacy claims must be verifiable via DevTools Network tab inspection
Privacy features must work across Chrome, Firefox, Safari (no browser-specific APIs)

interface.name: showPrivacyReminder()
interface.description: Displays privacy reminder message after file upload. Message auto-fades after 5 seconds. Called when fileLoaded event received.

interface.name: showConversionPrivacyMessage()
interface.description: Appends privacy reminder to conversion success message. Emphasizes browser-based processing. Called when conversionComplete event received.

interface.name: initializePrivacyFAQ()
interface.description: Initializes Privacy FAQ toggle functionality and privacy badge click handler. Called on DOMContentLoaded.

interface.name: togglePrivacyFAQ()
interface.description: Toggles Privacy FAQ section visibility (expand/collapse). Updates toggle button text.

interface.name: fileLoaded event
interface.description: Dispatched when file successfully uploaded. This story listens for this event to show privacy reminder.

interface.name: conversionComplete event
interface.description: Dispatched when conversion succeeds. This story listens for this event to show privacy message in success notification.

standards.standard: Manual testing in browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
Privacy verification via DevTools Network tab (no tracking requests)
Visual verification of privacy badge, reminders, FAQ section
Privacy FAQ accuracy review (technical claims match implementation)
User testing with non-technical users (photographers) to verify message clarity
External link validation (GitHub repository, WebAssembly explainer)
Auto-fade timing verification (privacy reminders disappear after 5 seconds)
Accessibility testing (FAQ toggle, privacy badge link, keyboard navigation)

locations.location: web/static/privacy-messaging.js (unit tests can be added inline or separate test file)
DevTools Network tab (verify zero tracking/analytics requests)
DevTools Application tab (verify no cookies, no local storage)

ideas.idea: Test privacy badge visibility: Load page, verify badge appears in header with lock icon and text
Test privacy badge click: Click badge, verify scrolls to Privacy FAQ section, FAQ auto-expands if collapsed
Test upload privacy reminder: Upload file, verify message appears "✓ File loaded. Processing locally...", fades after 5s
Test reminder non-intrusive: Verify message doesn't block other UI elements, doesn't interrupt workflow
Test conversion privacy message: Complete conversion, verify success includes "...converted entirely in your browser"
Test Privacy FAQ toggle: Click "Privacy FAQ ▼", verify section expands, click "Hide Privacy FAQ ▲", verify collapses
Test FAQ content: Read all 6 Q&A items, verify accuracy, no technical jargon, plain language
Test WASM explanation: Read WASM section in FAQ, verify understandable to non-technical users
Test WASM external link: Click "Learn more about WebAssembly" link, verify opens https://webassembly.org/ in new tab
Test GitHub link: Click "View on GitHub" in footer, verify opens Recipe repository in new tab
Test repository access: Verify repository is public and accessible (no 404 error)
Test no tracking (critical): Open DevTools Network tab, load page, upload file, convert, download, verify zero requests to analytics domains
Test no cookies: Check DevTools Application → Cookies, verify no cookies set
Test no storage: Check DevTools Application → Local Storage/Session Storage, verify empty
Test privacy policy (if implemented): Click "Privacy Policy" link, verify page loads with accurate content
Test user understanding: Show privacy badge and FAQ to 3 photographers, ask "Does Recipe upload your files?" (answer: No)
Test user understanding: Ask "Where does conversion happen?" (answer: In my browser/on my computer)
Test user understanding: Ask "Can Recipe access your files without permission?" (answer: No, only files I upload)
Test fade animation: Upload file, use stopwatch to verify privacy reminder fades after exactly 5 seconds
Test multiple uploads: Upload 3 files in sequence, verify privacy reminder shows each time, doesn't persist between uploads
Test browser compatibility: Run all tests in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, verify identical behavior
Test accessibility: Use keyboard only (Tab, Enter, Space) to navigate privacy badge, FAQ toggle, external links
Test mobile responsive: View on mobile device, verify privacy badge readable, FAQ toggle works, links functional
